query: I have an incident corpus with about 12,000 records merged from AIID, OECD AIM, and AIAAIC. Each source uses its own ID format (e.g. one source uses AIID-N, another uses AIID-N-OECD for the same incident). After ingest I see duplicates. How should I dedupe?
expected_behavior: Recommends multi-key dedup with an ordered list of rules and documented confidence per rule (not a single fuzzy similarity threshold)
Explicitly names ID-format normalization (stripping source suffixes like -OECD) as a required pre-processing step before the rules fire
Recommends connected-components / union-find on a duplicate graph to handle the transitive case, NOT greedy pairwise merging
